# User Story - US_031

## Story ID
   * US_031

## Story Title
   * Unified Patient Profile Generation

## Description
  * As a clinical staff member, I want an automatically generated unified patient profile aggregating data from all sources (intake forms, uploaded documents, manual entries), so that I have a complete patient history in one consolidated view.

## Acceptance Criteria
  1. **Given** a patient has completed intake and/or uploaded documents that have been processed, **When** I access the patient's clinical data review page (SCR-010), **Then** the system generates unified profile by aggregating data from all ClinicalDocuments entries for this patient_id, merging deduplicated data from US-030, prioritizing most recent values for changeable fields (current medications, address), preserving historical timeline for lab results and diagnoses, organizing into sections (Demographics, Chief Complaint, Medical History with dates, Current Medications with last updated date, Allergies with severity, Lab Results with trend graphs, Previous Visits), highlighting data conflicts in yellow with source document links, displaying confidence scores for AI-extracted fields, and providing "Last Updated" timestamp per section showing data freshness.

## Edge Cases
   * What happens when profile has no uploaded documents yet? (Show intake form data only, display "No documents uploaded" notice with "Upload Documents" button)
   * How are outdated records handled? (Medication from >1 year ago shows "Outdated?" warnin, suggest staff confirm with patient)
   * What if multiple sources have different DOB? (Flag critical data mismatches with red alert "Critical: Date of birth mismatch across documents")

## Traceability
### Parent Epic
    * Epic : EP-006

### Requirement Tags
    * FR-007 (Unified, de-duplicated patient profile with conflict highlighting)
    * AIR-003 (Patient profile synthesis)
    * AIR-S01 (Profile conflict detection >95%)
    * UC-003 (Clinical Data Aggregation)
    * UC-010 (View Unified Patient Profile)

### Dependencies
    * US-007 (PatientProfiles table)
    * US-029 (Document extraction)
    * US-030 (Deduplication)

## Visual Design Context (UI Stories Only)
### Screen Specifications
- **Screen ID(s)**: SCR-010 (Clinical Data Review)
- **Figma Spec Reference**: .propel/context/docs/figma_spec.md#SCR-010

### Wireframe References
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Status** | AVAILABLE |
| **Type** | HTML |
| **Path/URL** | .propel/context/wireframes/Hi-Fi/wireframe-SCR-010-clinical-data-review.html |

> **Wireframe Details (SCR-010 - Unified Profile View):**
> - **Profile header**: Patient photo, name, MRN, DOB, age, last visit date, "Last Updated: [timestamp]"
> - **Section tabs**: Demographics, Medical History, Medications, Allergies, Lab Results, Visits, Documents
> - **Conflict highlighting**: Yellow background on conflicting fields, "View Sources" dropdown shows documents with differing values
> - **Data source indicators**: Small document icons next to each data point with tooltip showing source document and extraction date
> - **Timeline view**: Lab results show trend lines, historical medication changes on timeline
> - **Confidence badges**: AI-extracted data shows confidence % (green >90%, yellow 80-90%)

### UX Requirements
- **UXR Mappings**: AIR-S01 (Conflict detection >95%), UXR-002 (Clear visual hierarchy), UXR-502 (Clear conflict indicators)
